title: tetrachyron grayi
intro: Tetrachyron grayi, also known as the Gray's tetrachyron or Gray's four-angled, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is a shrub native to Southeast Asia with medicinal properties attributed to its roots, leaves, and stems. The plant's roots have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Tetrachyron grayi can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and its herbal preparations have been used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
